pi_items.source: NinjaTrader 8 documentation and forums confirm SampleMACrossover is a built-in sample strategy
All three data files exist with 502 lines of historical OHLCV data each
First trading day in 2024 data is January 2, 2024 (January 1, 2024 was New Year's Day when markets were closed)
Last data record in all three files is dated 2024-12-31 (20241231 in yyyymmdd format)
Data contains 252 records for 2024 (standard trading days per year), one record per trading day in yyyymmdd;open;high;low;close;volume format

Environment description in env.json states 'real Yahoo Finance data (SPY, AAPL, MSFT 2023-2024)'. Import script references Yahoo Finance. Data format (yyyymmdd;OHLCV) matches NinjaTrader's historical data import format.
Data files contain 502 records: first record dated 20230103, last record dated 20241231
Cannot verify without access to Windows environment
Cannot verify without access to Windows environment; referenced in verifier.py but script does not exist in codebase yet

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real Yahoo Finance historical data for SPY, AAPL, and MSFT covering approximately 2 years (January 3, 2023 to December 31, 2024). The data is in daily OHLCV format with 252 trading records per year. The SampleMACrossover strategy is a built-in, open-source sample strategy included with NinjaTrader 8 designed for educational purposes as a moving average crossover strategy. The metadata claims a backtest date range starting on 2024-01-01, but the actual first available trading day in the data is 2024-01-02 (since January 1, 2024 was New Year's Day when US markets were closed). The data ends on the claimed date of 2024-12-31.
